comments: The first use of 'Theme One' for the Friday Night Connection
From a collection of 274 tapes mixing John Peel shows, Friday Rock Shows and Alan Freeman Saturday Shows from 1978 to 1983. The Derby Box was acquired by the John Peel Yahoo Group.

track_listing.details: LP-If You Can't Stand The Heat; Vertigo

track_listing.track: 'Axe Victim' (2xLP-The Best Of And The Rest Of Be Bop Deluxe) (Harvest). Originally on (LP-Axe Victim) (Harvest)

track_listing.details: LP-The Wonderful World Of Wreckless Eric; Stiff

transcript.text: On 275 and 285, in the medium wave and stereo VHF, this is Radio 1 Nationwide.
Hi there, good evening, this is Tommy Vance. Welcome to the Friday Rock Show.
And tonight we have the latest session by The Flies and one by Lone Star out of the archives, recorded 29 the 1st of 76.
We've got some classic tracks by Hendrix, Manassas, Kraftwerk and David Bowie.
New cuts by Kirby, Reckless Eric, Gary Moore, Van Morrison, and Judas Priest.
Also, we'll have a Friday Night Connection as set by last week's winner of the £10 record voucher.
But tonight, we start off with UFO.
That's a guy who used to be in Elmer Gantry's Velvet Opera some time ago when they were around,
and also the band called Stretch, a gentleman by the name of Kirby,
in a track from an LP called Composition.
It's out on the Hot Wax label, and a tasty bit of music it is too.
Before that, we played something called Hot and Ready from the LP Obsessions by UFO,
which is out on Chrysalis.
And we played it because we were asked to do so,
by Penny Reynolds of Small Heath in Birmingham.
Hello, Penny.
If anybody else would like to hear anything on the programme,
just write to me.
Tommy Vance, The Friday Rock Show, Radio 1, BBC, London, W1A4WW.
And now, back to 1976 and the session by Lone Star.
Produced by Tony Wilson way back in January of 1976.
A nice bit of archive material there from Lone Star.
And if you were listening in stereo, and I hope you were,
you must have heard the beautiful interplay in the guitars there.
Incidentally, that was called Fly Into The Real,
and if you want the recorded version, it's on the LP Lone Star.
They recorded it in the BBC studios
way before they ever did their deal with CBS.
BBC Radio 1, first again.
Dave Barber, 20, Ilsham Road, Torquay and Devon,
this is going to bring it back to you.
It's good. It was very good indeed.
It was just a shame that he had to leave.
I'll never forget sitting in this very, very studio the day that he died,
and in something like 25 minutes to go before transmission time,
on a programme that I think was called What's New in those days,
putting together an obituary to that guy.
I don't know how I got through it, and I never will.
A wonderful guy.
That was from the album Are You Experienced?
And it was, of course, that incredible character by the name of Hendrix,
especially for Dave Barber, down there in Torquay in Devon.
Right, studio session group number two, The Flies.
Quite amazing to remember that they were actually formed way back in 1962,
and they've had virtually the same line-up, I think, since 1966.
That was Status Quo from the album If You Can't Stand the Heat.
The track was accident-prone.
It's also released, as if you didn't know, as a single.
And before that, you heard the lead track from the LP Waikiki Beach Refugees by The Flies,
though we played it as a studio session.
I was chatting to a couple of The Flies the other day,
and they one time opened up their own club in Coventry, their hometown, just to get some exposure.
Now that's getting it together.
Here's some Van Morrison.
Would you get me on?
And the title track from the album called Wavelength.
It was recorded, incidentally, in the Manor in Oxfordshire.
And it features people like Peter Bardens from Camel
and Bobby Tench who plays with a band called Hummingbird.
Have you ever heard of them?
They've got a really tasty album.
Two albums, I think, in fact.
Anyway, not to digress too much.
If you ever want a great sort of burst of torrid language in Welsh,
just ask Dave Edmonds what he thinks of Van Morrison.
It's quite amusing.
Can't understand a word, but you can understand what he's implying.
Here's a classic track now from Stephen Stills' Manassas.
The unit they were when they were out.
Stephen Stills' Manassas from the double album that came out way back in 1972.
Do you recollect that track that Stills did on one of his albums, Black Queen?
Do you remember that track?
Must dig it out one day and play it.
Now, the Friday Rock Show.
Meanwhile, on Radio 3...
I went out to this party last night, you know, there was all there.
My mate was there, John, John Peel, do you know him, dear?
Oh, he's a good mate of mine.
Well, he asked me to get out of the way once, you know what I mean?
I mean, but there was all there, there was...
from the radio one studios they had the flies and a thing called name dropping something that we all
do every once in a while mind you if it's a name like john peel it just bounces back at you monday
Tonight, the John Peel Show will feature Sessions by Aswad and XTC.
Here's Bebop Deluxe.
...that I sung so clear...
...and he's getting his wish most of the time, unfortunately.
That's from The Wonderful World of Reckless Eric, his recent album.
The track was I Wish It Would Rain.
And before that, you heard a track called Axe Victim,
which was the title of the very first Harvest album that Bebop Deluxe put out.
It's from a new double set that's available called The Best of the Rest of Bebop Deluxe.
It's exactly 11 o'clock.
And here, our Lone Star.
Lone Star, and the second of three really good tracks we've got on the programme tonight.
That was called A Million Stars.
If you're using the A11 road in Cambridgeshire, this is for you.
An accident has completely blocked the A11 road at Worsted Lodge, north of the A604 intersection, about ten miles south of Newmarket.
This section of the A11 will remain closed for two to three hours, and drivers should steer well clear of the area.
And in West London, a serious accident has brought heavy congestion on the A40 Western Avenue at Acton.
Use another route in and out of London for the next hour or so.
Please, be a safe driver.
It could be me that you hit.
Hey, now it's time for the Friday Night Connection.
We can't fox you.
You're too clever for us.
I would say that 98% of all the cars that were sent in were right about last week's connection.
The tracks were Carry On, Wayward Son, If You Leave Me Now, and More Than a Feeling.
They were by Kansas, Chicago, and Boston.
All names of American cities.
You smart lot.
Now I'm going to play you three bits of music, total length 11 minutes and 55 seconds.
There is a very definite theme running through these three pieces of music
that have been put together by P. Redgrave of 15 Litchfield Terrace,
Cranham, I think it is, up Minster in Essex.
Mr. Redgrave, because you're so smart, you deserve the £10 record voucher that we're going to send to you.
And if anybody out there can guess what the theme is to these three pieces of music, then write it down on a postcard and put together your own Friday Night Connection.
Are you ready? Are you sitting comfortably? Good.
That's a little bit harder this week.
This week's Friday Night Connection was put together by P. Redgrave of No. 15 Litchfield Terrace, Cranham, Upminster and Essex.
He gets the £10 voucher because he got the right answer last week and put this one together.
Now, if you can do the same, I'd be very grateful. Really.
Please tell me what the connection is between those three pieces of music
and, at the same time, develop your own Friday Night Connection for next week.
Plug it on a postcard and send it to Friday Night Connection,
BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
Go on, share your music.
Here's a new track by Gary Moore.
That man used to be in Thin Lizzy.
In fact, it is a Phil Lennott song, and Phil Lennott's on it as well.
The name of the man is Gary Moore.
the name of the album is Back on the Streets
the track was called Fanatical Fascists
by type of person at all
you'll find it on MCA
we did play that off the album a couple of weeks ago I think
to the best of my recollection which is never that good
but that was the Flyers from our studios
and a thing called I Don't Know
you'll find it on the album Waikiki Beach
Refugees
stay tuned for some Jean-Luc Ponti
rock on this weekend
Alice Cooper and Bernie Taupin
on the subject of drinking, drying out
and deriving an album from the experience
Also, Jim Morrison lives.
Surviving Doors, Robbie Krieger, Ray Manzarek and John Densmore
bring you live to their one-time leader
with a unique album of poetry and music, An American Prayer.
Then there's Rock On's retrospective on a year of albums,
some favourite choices and the reasons why.
Rock On this weekend, then.
Alice Cooper, Bernie Taupin and The Doors.
Saturday, 4.31. Rock On.
Nice to hear that again.
Came out originally in 1976.
It was on the album Imaginary Voyage, Jean-Luc Ponty and Tarantula,
especially for Bob Hawkins, who asked for it.
Bob is at number 68, Bagnall Avenue, I think it is,
Oxclose Lane Estate, Arnold in Nottinghamshire.
If anybody else would like to hear anything, Simplicity Personified.
Put it on a postcard, send it to me, Tommy Vance,
The Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4, WW.
And I'll get Tony Wilson to read it for me, because I can't read.
A couple of classic tracks.
That was the live version of Fame from the LP Stage,
written by John Lennon and David Bowie.
Nice.
Before that, Beyond the Realms of Death
from the LP Stained Glass by Judas Priest,
requested by Nick Jackson
of 47 Parrotview, New Malden, in Surrey.
Here's a request.
It's mine, and I hope you don't mind.
Father sends his regards to you
will i write well once in a while i'll send my love and the molotov cocktail too
and that's our final track from the flyers very nice session gentlemen it was called love and a
molotov cocktail and here's our final cut from the 76 recording done in our studios by lone star
this is the song she said absolutely beautiful she said as recorded back in 1976 that was lone
star the session was requested again by tony wheelan of birmingham also by dave ross of london
nw2 and anthony leake of chard in somerset the fly session was produced by mike robinson lone
star was produced by tony wilson tony wilson produces the whole shebang now whatever you're
to do this weekend do it well and thoroughly enjoy yourself peely on monday we'll be presenting
asswabbed and xtc i'll see you next week at the same time till then from me take care
